fields.summary: Add support for NM Recovery of assigned resources (e.g. GPU's, NUMA, FPGA's) to container

issuetype.description: An improvement or enhancement to an existing feature or task.
issuetype.name: Improvement

fields.description: This JIRA adds the common logic to store the assigned resources to container such as GPU's(YARN-6620), NUMA(YARN-5764) and FPGA's(YARN-5983) etc. and recover upon restart of NM.

resolution.description: A fix for this issue is checked into the tree and tested.
resolution.name: Fixed

fields.summary: Port YARN-7033 NM recovery of assigned resources to branch-3.0/branch-2

status.description: A resolution has been taken, and it is awaiting verification by reporter. From here issues are either reopened, or are closed.
status.name: Resolved

fields.summary: Add support in NodeManager to isolate GPU devices by using CGroups

status.description: A resolution has been taken, and it is awaiting verification by reporter. From here issues are either reopened, or are closed.
status.name: Resolved

fields.summary: NUMA awareness support for launching containers

status.description: A resolution has been taken, and it is awaiting verification by reporter. From here issues are either reopened, or are closed.
status.name: Resolved

issuetype.description: A new feature of the product, which has yet to be developed.
issuetype.name: New Feature

comments.body: Attaching the patch which contains the common code from YARN-6620 to handle the assigned resources recovery.

This message was automatically generated.

Thanks [~devaraj.k], +1 from my side, I would request another set of eyes to take a look at the patch.

[~sunilg] could you help to review the patch?

Thanks [~devaraj.k] and [~leftnoteasy]

Some quick comments:
# In Container or ContainerImpl, its better to add a javadoc for *getResourceMappings*
# One doubt about {{storeAssignedResources}}, in which event this method ll be invoked ?
# In general {{String resourceType}} could be an ENUM so that it may be easier to operate, thoughts?

Thanks [~leftnoteasy] and [~sunilg] for the reviews.

bq. In Container or ContainerImpl, its better to add a javadoc for getResourceMappings
I have added the javadoc for Container and ContainerImpl getResourceMappings.
bq. One doubt about storeAssignedResources, in which event this method ll be invoked ?
This method would be called from the assign/allocate of the Resource Allocaor implementations. 
bq. In general String resourceType could be an ENUM so that it may be easier to operate, thoughts?
I made the changes to use enum instead of String.

And also I changed AssignedResources.resources type from List<String> to List<Serializable> to support other types of Serializable as well.

[~devaraj.k],
Apologize I missed last comment from [~sunilg]. I suggest not change type from String to enum, we need to change code when any new resource type added to the system. And after YARN-3926, it is possible to support arbitrary resource type and enforce it in a customized resource module, enum doesn't work in this case. 

Thoughts? +[~sunilg].

Sigh. Thats on me. 
I thoughts this recovery model is only for GPU/NUMA/FPGA. So post YARN-3926, any arbitrary new resource could be considered here. [~leftnoteasy]
Thanks [~leftnoteasy] and [~sunilg] for the confirmation, I will update the patch with the revert of enum change.

This message was automatically generated.
[~sunilg], can you check the latest patch? Thanks

Thanks [~devaraj.k] for the patch.
One last comment, In *NMLeveldbStateStoreService*, CONTAINER_ASSIGNED_RESOURCES_KEY_SUFFIX will be updated only in case of GPU's, NUMA, FPGA's cases correct. If some one adds a custom resource after YARN-3926, will this code hit ?

[~sunilg], Thanks again for looking into this.

bq. In NMLeveldbStateStoreService, CONTAINER_ASSIGNED_RESOURCES_KEY_SUFFIX will be updated only in case of GPU's, NUMA, FPGA's cases correct. If some one adds a custom resource after YARN-3926, will this code hit ?
Here CONTAINER_ASSIGNED_RESOURCES_KEY_SUFFIX is combined with the resourceType and is used as key for each container assigned resources of that particular type, It should work for any resourceType. There is nothing to bind only for these GPU's, NUMA, FPGA's types. Please let me know if doesn't clarifies or any other thoughts.
[~sunilg], IIUC, there's no hardcoded logic for {{CONTAINER_ASSIGNED_RESOURCES_KEY_SUFFIX}}, please let me know if I understand wrong.
Yes. Thanks for the explanation [~devaraj.k] and [~leftnoteasy]. +1

Thanks [~sunilg] for the review and agreeing with us. 

[~leftnoteasy], can you commit this if you don't have any further comments? Thanks
Committed to trunk, thanks [~devaraj.k] and thanks reviews from [~sunilg]!

This message was automatically generated.

Thanks [~devaraj.k], +1 from my side, I would request another set of eyes to take a look at the patch.

[~sunilg] could you help to review the patch?

Thanks [~devaraj.k] and [~leftnoteasy]

Some quick comments:
# In Container or ContainerImpl, its better to add a javadoc for *getResourceMappings*
# One doubt about {{storeAssignedResources}}, in which event this method ll be invoked ?
# In general {{String resourceType}} could be an ENUM so that it may be easier to operate, thoughts?

Thanks [~leftnoteasy] and [~sunilg] for the reviews.

bq. In Container or ContainerImpl, its better to add a javadoc for getResourceMappings
I have added the javadoc for Container and ContainerImpl getResourceMappings.
bq. One doubt about storeAssignedResources, in which event this method ll be invoked ?
This method would be called from the assign/allocate of the Resource Allocaor implementations. 
bq. In general String resourceType could be an ENUM so that it may be easier to operate, thoughts?
I made the changes to use enum instead of String.

And also I changed AssignedResources.resources type from List<String> to List<Serializable> to support other types of Serializable as well.

[~devaraj.k],
Apologize I missed last comment from [~sunilg]. I suggest not change type from String to enum, we need to change code when any new resource type added to the system. And after YARN-3926, it is possible to support arbitrary resource type and enforce it in a customized resource module, enum doesn't work in this case. 

Thoughts? +[~sunilg].

[~sunilg], can you check the latest patch? Thanks

Thanks [~devaraj.k] for the patch.
One last comment, In *NMLeveldbStateStoreService*, CONTAINER_ASSIGNED_RESOURCES_KEY_SUFFIX will be updated only in case of GPU's, NUMA, FPGA's cases correct. If some one adds a custom resource after YARN-3926, will this code hit ?

[~sunilg], Thanks again for looking into this.

bq. In NMLeveldbStateStoreService, CONTAINER_ASSIGNED_RESOURCES_KEY_SUFFIX will be updated only in case of GPU's, NUMA, FPGA's cases correct. If some one adds a custom resource after YARN-3926, will this code hit ?
Here CONTAINER_ASSIGNED_RESOURCES_KEY_SUFFIX is combined with the resourceType and is used as key for each container assigned resources of that particular type, It should work for any resourceType. There is nothing to bind only for these GPU's, NUMA, FPGA's types. Please let me know if doesn't clarifies or any other thoughts.
[~sunilg], IIUC, there's no hardcoded logic for {{CONTAINER_ASSIGNED_RESOURCES_KEY_SUFFIX}}, please let me know if I understand wrong.
Yes. Thanks for the explanation [~devaraj.k] and [~leftnoteasy]. +1

Thanks [~sunilg] for the review and agreeing with us. 

[~leftnoteasy], can you commit this if you don't have any further comments? Thanks
Committed to trunk, thanks [~devaraj.k] and thanks reviews from [~sunilg]!
